request.context: Version 2026.05.31 was deployed at 10:00. Checkout error rate rose from 1% to 18% after deployment, and logs identify a regression in version v17.
request.rationale: Because version v17 was deployed at 10:00 and checkout error rate rose from 1% to 18% after that deployment, rolling back the deployment targets the regression.

request.context: No deployment occurred today. Version v17 has been stable for a week. Checkout error rate is below 1%, while latency alerts come from a downstream vendor timeout.
request.rationale: Because version v17 was deployed at 10:00 and checkout error rate rose from 1% to 18% after that deployment, rolling back the deployment targets the regression.

request.context: Postgres reports 14 idle in transaction sessions older than 45 minutes holding locks on invoices. VACUUM and checkout writes are blocked.
request.rationale: Because 14 idle sessions older than 45 minutes are holding locks on invoices and blocking writes, terminating idle sessions releases the locks.

request.context: Postgres latency is high, but the note does not mention sessions, transaction age, locks, invoices, VACUUM, or blocked writes.
request.rationale: Because 14 idle sessions older than 45 minutes are holding locks on invoices and blocking writes, terminating idle sessions releases the locks.

request.context: Queue workers show CPU saturation at 96%, memory pressure is rising, p95 latency is 900 ms, and no deployment occurred.
request.rationale: Because CPU saturation is 96%, memory pressure is rising, and p95 latency is 900 ms, scaling service replicas adds capacity.

request.context: Queue workers show CPU normal at 24%, memory pressure is absent, p95 latency is 120 ms, and capacity is healthy.
request.rationale: Because CPU saturation is 96%, memory pressure is rising, and p95 latency is 900 ms, scaling service replicas adds capacity.

request.context: Database disk I/O wait is high at 92%, storage IOPS are exhausted, and checkout writes are delayed by the storage bottleneck.
request.rationale: Because disk I/O wait is high at 92% and storage IOPS are exhausted, increasing IOPS addresses the storage bottleneck.

request.context: Database disk I/O is normal at 8%, storage IOPS are healthy, and checkout writes are delayed by an application lock.
request.rationale: Because disk I/O wait is high at 92% and storage IOPS are exhausted, increasing IOPS addresses the storage bottleneck.

request.context: Worker pods show memory growth from 800 MB to 3 GB, heap pressure is high, and OOM restarts are beginning after a cache leak.
request.rationale: Because memory grew from 800 MB to 3 GB and heap pressure is high from a cache leak, restarting service pods clears the leaked state.

request.context: Worker pods show memory healthy at 800 MB, heap pressure is absent, and no OOM restarts or cache leak are present.
request.rationale: Because memory grew from 800 MB to 3 GB and heap pressure is high from a cache leak, restarting service pods clears the leaked state.
